name: Unbounded restart loops for noncritical services
summary: Restarting noncritical services aggressively can create needless churn and repeated work.
description: Compose configurations that restart transient or low-value services indefinitely can waste local resources and hide operational problems instead of surfacing them.

impact.cost: Consumes unnecessary local or CI resources.
impact.performance: Repeated restarts create churn and noise.
impact.carbon: Repeated failed restarts waste compute cycles.

remediation.guidance: Use restart policies intentionally and avoid infinite restart churn for services that should fail fast during development.
examples: Prefer clearer failure behavior for optional tooling containers.
